page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highcpu-16 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 30.0 GB of system memory, which translates to a resource density of 1.88 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include any local storage or integrated GPU accelerators, requiring reliance on network-attached storage for persistent data.

The hardware profile of this server is designed for compute-heavy workloads where memory requirements are minimal. By minimizing the memory-to-CPU ratio, the instance provides a cost-effective allocation of dedicated compute resources without the added expense of unutilized RAM. However, the absence of local NVMe storage and hardware accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, making it unsuitable for IOPS-intensive databases or deep learning tasks.

This server is best suited for workloads such as web serving, batch processing, continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, and distributed compute tasks. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance for multi-threaded application servers and computational modeling that fit within the 30.0 GB memory boundary.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 30.0 GB of RAM. With a memory-to-core ratio of 1.88 GB per vCPU, this instance is designed to deliver cost-efficient compute power for workloads that do not require large memory footprints. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, meaning all storage must be network-attached. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance, making it highly suitable for compute-bound tasks such as web serving, batch processing, and CI/CD pipelines.
